REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT SAM CHOY, YI CHANG DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINTIS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS CROSS-COMPLAINT PURPORTS TO ALLEGE 4 CAUSES OF ACTION, ALL OF WHICH ARISE OUT THE CLAIM THAT CROSS-COMPLAINT HAS INTERTEST IN THE SUBJECT PROPERTY. CENTRAL TO CROSS-COMPLAINT'S CLAIMS IS THE ALLEGATION IN PARAGRAPH 23 OF THE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. HER TITLE CLAIMS ARISE OUT OF (1) CLAIMS TO THE DOWN PAYMENT AMOUNTS OBTAINED FROM FAMILY RESIDENCE PRIOR TO 2005; AND/OR (2) PURPORTED MORTGAGE AND TAX PAYMENT CONTRIBUTIONS TOWARDS THE SUBJECT PROPERTY SINCE MAY 2005. IN THE DEMURRER MOVING PARTY RAISED THE STATUE OF LIMITATIONS ISSUE, WHICH IS ADDRESSED IN OPPOSITION IN A 2-SENTESE STATEMENT AT 2:12-13. THIS OPPOSITION DOES NOT ADDRESS CAUSES OF ACTION 1-3 AND PURPORTS TO REFERENCE "RECENT EVENTS." THE DOWN PAYMENT ISSUE AROSE PRIOR TO 2005, NO EXPLANATION IS PROVIDED WHY PRE-2005 EVENTS ARE RECENT. FURTHERMORE, CROSS-COMPLAINANT SEEKS TO QUIET TITLE AS OF APRIL 14, 2004. NO "RECENT EVENTS" COULD CONTRIBUTE TO THE DETERMINATIONS REGARDING THE STATUE OF THE TITLE ON APRIL 14, 2004. FINALLY, OPPOSITION CITES NO AUTHORITY AS TO WHEN THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS STARTS AND EXPIRES IF PARTITION/ACCOUNTING CLAIM IS BASED ON CONTRIBUTIONS TOWARDS THE PROPERTY DATING BACK TO 2005. LACK OF OPPOSITION ON THESE ISSUES CONCEDES THE POINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, REPORTED (D501)
ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER OF CROSS-DEFENDANTS TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND NO SASE. NO ENDORSED FILED COPY.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS HEREBY DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE AND THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT TAKES NOTHING AS AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANTS, AND EACH OF THEM. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT FOR DETAILS.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-21-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-02-2020 Continued To May-24-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
Elsa Baldwin, Esq., 510-548-7474, appearing for Jackie Chang.
Camilo Artiga-Purcell, Esq., 415-226-7651, appearing for Samy Choy
Court reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR No: 13714, Telephone: (415) 601-7054

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). Ms. Baldwin reported that she contested to the tenative ruling and provided case background. Mr. Artiga-Purcell provided oral arguement in support of his postion.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT SAM CHOY, YI CHANG DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINTis SUSTAINED without leave to amend. This cross-complaint purports to allege 4 causes of action, all of which arise out the claim that Cross-Complaint has intertest in the Subject Property. Central to Cross-Complaint's claims is the allegation in paragraph 23 of the Second Amended Cross-Complaint. Her title claims arise out of (1) claims to the down payment amounts obtained from Family Residence prior to 2005; and/or (2) purported mortgage and tax payment contributions towards the Subject property since May 2005. In the demurrer moving party raised the statue of limitations issue, which is addressed in opposition in a 2-sentese statement at 2:12-13. This opposition does not address causes of action 1-3 and purports to reference "recent events." The down payment issue arose prior to 2005, no explanation is provided why pre-2005 events are recent. Furthermore, Cross-Complainant seeks to quiet title as of April 14, 2004. No "recent events" could contribute to the determinations regarding the statue of the title on April 14, 2004. Finally, opposition cites no authority as to when the statute of limitations starts and expires if partition/accounting claim is based on contributions towards the property dating back to 2005. Lack of opposition on these issues concedes the point. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Reported (D501)
